events.title: INCOSPAR founded
events.detail: Vikram Sarabhai persuades the government to form the Indian National Committee for Space Research, backed by Homi Bhabha.

events.title: First sounding rocket launched from Thumba
events.detail: A US-supplied Nike-Apache lifts off from a new launch station near Thiruvananthapuram, chosen for its position on the geomagnetic equator.

events.title: ISRO formally established
events.detail: The Indian Space Research Organisation supersedes INCOSPAR, still under Sarabhai's leadership.

events.title: Vikram Sarabhai dies suddenly
events.detail: India's space programme loses its founder at age 52.

events.title: Department of Space created
events.detail: Satish Dhawan becomes ISRO chairman; the administrative shape ISRO still has today takes hold.

events.title: Aryabhata launched
events.detail: India's first satellite reaches orbit on a Soviet rocket from Kapustin Yar — India has no orbital launcher of its own yet.

events.title: Bhaskara-1 launched
events.detail: An experimental Earth-observation satellite follows Aryabhata.

events.title: SLV-3 reaches orbit
events.detail: India's first indigenously built launch vehicle places the Rohini satellite into orbit from Sriharikota — the moment India stops needing someone else's rocket.

events.title: APPLE launched
events.detail: India's first geostationary communications satellite reaches orbit via an Ariane rocket.

events.title: ASLV begins flight-testing
events.detail: A rougher run through 1994, but it feeds directly into the propulsion and guidance knowledge that makes PSLV possible.

events.title: INSAT-1B begins operational service
events.detail: India's operational communications-satellite era begins — the ancestor of today's INSAT/GSAT family.

events.title: Rakesh Sharma becomes the first Indian in space
events.detail: He flies aboard Soyuz T-11 to the Salyut 7 space station under an Indo-Soviet agreement.

events.title: IRS-1A begins the Indian Remote Sensing programme
events.detail: Ancestor of today's Resourcesat and Cartosat families.

events.title: PSLV's debut flight fails
events.detail: PSLV-D1 does not reach orbit.

events.title: PSLV-D2 succeeds
events.detail: The workhorse era begins — this vehicle will go on to carry nearly every major ISRO planetary mission.

events.title: First GSLV launch
events.detail: Built to carry heavier communications satellites than PSLV could manage.

events.title: Chandrayaan-1 launched
events.detail: India's first mission beyond Earth orbit; its Moon Impact Probe strikes the lunar surface near the south pole that November.

events.title: Water confirmed on the Moon
events.detail: Chandrayaan-1's NASA-supplied Moon Mineralogy Mapper finds widespread evidence of water molecules across the lunar surface.

events.title: Mars Orbiter Mission launched
events.detail: Mangalyaan begins its journey to Mars.

events.title: Mangalyaan reaches Mars orbit
events.detail: On its first attempt — India becomes the first Asian nation to reach Mars.

events.title: LVM3's first test flight
events.detail: A sub-orbital test of the rocket that will later carry Gaganyaan's human-rated missions.

events.title: Astrosat launched
events.detail: India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory, watching in visible, ultraviolet, and X-ray bands at once.

events.title: PSLV-C37 launches 104 satellites
events.detail: A single-mission world record at the time.

events.title: Gaganyaan announced
events.detail: ISRO's own human-spaceflight programme is announced in an Independence Day address.

events.title: Chandrayaan-2 launched
events.detail: The orbiter succeeds; the Vikram lander loses contact roughly 2.1 km above the surface during descent on 6–7 September 2019.

events.title: IN-SPACe founded
events.detail: Opens the launch and satellite sector to private Indian companies.

events.title: Vikram-S flies
events.detail: Skyroot Aerospace's suborbital rocket becomes India's first privately built launch vehicle to fly; SSLV debuts the same year.

events.title: Gaganyaan TV-D1 abort test succeeds
events.detail: A crew-escape-system test — one of the careful, uncrewed steps toward a crewed flight.

events.title: Aditya-L1 launched
events.detail: India's first solar mission begins its journey to the Sun-Earth L1 point.

events.title: Chandrayaan-3 soft-lands on the Moon
events.detail: Vikram lands near the lunar south pole at Shiv Shakti Point — the fourth country ever to soft-land on the Moon, the first this close to the pole. The date is now National Space Day.

events.title: Aditya-L1 reaches its halo orbit
events.detail: About 1.5 million km from Earth, at the Sun-Earth L1 point.

events.title: Four gaganyatris named
events.detail: IAF test pilots Prasanth Nair, Ajit Krishnan, Angad Pratap, and Shubhanshu Shukla are confirmed as Gaganyaan astronaut-designates.

events.title: Chandrayaan-4 gets Cabinet approval
events.detail: A ₹2,104.06 crore lunar sample-return mission requiring two LVM3 launches and in-orbit docking of five modules.

events.title: SpaDeX docking achieved
events.detail: Twin satellites launched by PSLV on 30 December 2024 dock successfully — India becomes the fourth nation to master orbital docking, a precursor technology Chandrayaan-4 will need.

events.title: PSLV third-stage anomaly — EOS-09
events.detail: Under review by an ISRO expert committee.

events.title: Shubhanshu Shukla reaches the ISS
events.detail: Flying aboard Axiom Mission 4, a commercial SpaceX Crew Dragon flight — the first Indian ISS visit, and only the second Indian ever in space. He returns 15 July 2025 after roughly 60 experiments.

events.title: NISAR launched
events.detail: The NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar satellite — the first to fly dual-frequency radar, with data made freely available worldwide within one to two days.

events.title: PSLV third-stage anomaly — EOS-N1
events.detail: A second consecutive anomaly, also under formal review.

events.title: Shubhanshu Shukla awarded the Ashoka Chakra
events.detail: India's highest peacetime gallantry decoration, conferred on the 77th Republic Day.

events.title: Vikram-1 launch window opens
events.detail: Skyroot Aerospace's "Mission Aagaman" — India's first private orbital launch attempt, window 12 July to 4 August 2026.

events.title: Gaganyaan G1 targeted
events.detail: An uncrewed orbital test flight carrying the humanoid stand-in Vyommitra, now officially targeted for the second half of 2026 after review following the EOS-N1 PSLV anomaly.

events.title: Gaganyaan H1 targeted
events.detail: The programme's first crewed flight, after G1, G2, and G3.

events.title: Chandrayaan-4 targeted
events.detail: Lunar sample-return, roughly 2–3 kg of material from near the south pole.

events.title: First Bharatiya Antariksh Station module targeted
events.detail: BAS-01, the first module of India's own space station.

events.title: Bharatiya Antariksh Station fully operational
events.detail: The planned completion date for India's space station.

events.title: Chandrayaan-5/LUPEX planned
events.detail: A joint mission with Japan's JAXA to send a rover to the lunar south pole hunting for water ice.

events.title: Shukrayaan-1 in planning
events.detail: A Venus Orbiter Mission in active planning, following a national science meeting on its objectives.

events.title: Crewed Moon landing (vision)
events.detail: ISRO leadership has publicly discussed a crewed Indian Moon landing around this target.
